skill.name: azure-keyvault-certificates-rust
skill.description: Guidance for using the Azure Key Vault Certificates Rust SDK to create, import, list, delete, and manage certificate policies.
skill.summary: Use the Azure Key Vault Certificates Rust SDK for certificate lifecycle tasks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell execution, hardcoded URLs, and a weak cryptography blocker. Review found no executable skill scripts, no prompt injection, and no malicious network behavior; the flagged shell and Rust snippets are documentation examples. The remaining concerns are low severity: a package installation command, public documentation links, and a placeholder certificate password in sample code.

low_findings.title: Package Installation Command in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill includes a cargo add command for installing Azure SDK dependencies. This is a normal setup instruction, but users should review dependency versions and project policy before running it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly visible in a shell code block and installs named Rust crates. It is not hidden execution and has no user-controlled interpolation.

low_findings.title: Public Network Endpoints and Reference Links
low_findings.description: The skill contains placeholder Azure Key Vault URLs and links to docs.rs, GitHub, and crates.io. These are expected references, not evidence of data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation and service endpoint examples. No code sends secrets to an unauthorized destination.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Certificate Password in Example
low_findings.description: The import certificate example uses a literal optional password placeholder. This is not a leaked secret, but examples should encourage secure secret loading instead of hardcoded passwords.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The value is clearly a placeholder string inside sample Rust code. The risk is educational rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Rust Code Blocks Parsed as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Most external command detections point to fenced Rust examples and method calls. They demonstrate SDK usage and do not execute commands from the skill itself.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown examples, not executable files or automation hooks. No dynamic command construction or hidden execution path is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Blocker
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at the description line, but the line only describes Azure Key Vault certificate management. No weak algorithm such as MD5, SHA-1 signing, DES, or RC4 is specified there.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is metadata and trigger language, not cryptographic implementation code. I found no evidence of a weak algorithm recommendation in that location.

content.value_statement: Rust developers need reliable examples for Azure Key Vault certificate operations. This skill provides focused SDK patterns for authentication, lifecycle tasks, and certificate policies.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install Azure Key Vault certificate and identity crates.
Demonstrates DeveloperToolsCredential authentication for local development.
Provides Rust examples for get, create, import, delete, and list operations.
Explains certificate policy retrieval and update patterns.
Summarizes certificate lifecycle steps and Azure RBAC roles.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not run Azure operations by itself.
It does not cover production managed identity configuration in depth.
It does not include complete error handling or retry strategy examples.
It uses placeholder vault names and certificate values.

use_cases.title: Add Certificate Operations to a Rust Service
use_cases.description: Use focused examples to wire Azure Key Vault certificate retrieval and listing into an application.

use_cases.title: Prototype Certificate Lifecycle Automation
use_cases.description: Create or import certificates, review policies, and map required SDK calls before production hardening.

use_cases.title: Review Azure RBAC Requirements
use_cases.description: Use the role guidance to align application permissions with Key Vault certificate tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up the SDK
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to add Azure Key Vault Certificates support to a Rust project using this skill.

prompt_templates.title: Read a Certificate
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Rust example that authenticates to Azure Key Vault and retrieves a certificate by name.

prompt_templates.title: Import a Certificate Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt the import certificate example so the certificate data and password are loaded securely, not hardcoded.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Lifecycle Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Rust certificate lifecycle workflow for create, policy update, expiration monitoring, and deletion using Azure Key Vault.

output_examples.input: I need to retrieve a Key Vault certificate from Rust.
output_examples.output: A concise Rust workflow that creates a CertificateClient, calls get_certificate, converts the response model, and prints safe certificate metadata.

output_examples.input: I need to import a PFX certificate into Azure Key Vault.
output_examples.output: Guidance for preparing import parameters, supplying certificate data and password securely, and calling import_certificate with clear error handling notes.

output_examples.input: I need to understand certificate permissions.
output_examples.output: A role-focused explanation that maps read-only metadata access to Key Vault Reader and full certificate operations to Key Vault Certificates Officer.

best_practices: Use Entra ID credentials and avoid embedding secrets in source code.
Prefer managed certificates and policy-based renewal where supported.
Monitor certificate expiration and keep soft delete enabled for production vaults.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode certificate passwords, vault URLs with tenant secrets, or private key material.
Do not grant broad certificate roles when read-only access is enough.
Do not treat sample code as production-ready without error handling and observability.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Azure commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and Rust SDK examples. Users decide what code or commands to run.

faq.question: Which Rust crate does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on azure_security_keyvault_certificates with azure_identity for authentication.

faq.question: Can it create certificates?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an example for create_certificate with a basic certificate policy.

faq.question: Can it import existing certificates?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows import_certificate parameters for base64 certificate data and an optional password.

faq.question: Is the password example production safe?
faq.answer: No. Treat it as a placeholder and load sensitive values from approved secret handling systems.

faq.question: Does it explain Azure permissions?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists Key Vault Certificates Officer for full certificate access and Key Vault Reader for metadata reads.
